toolUseResult.originalFile:
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.3
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.address=:443"
      - "--providers.file.directory=/data/dynamic"
      - "--providers.file.watch=true"
      - "--log.level=INFO"
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
      - ./.cert:/certs:ro
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic /data/certs
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data

        # Copy certificates
        cp /certs/cert.pem /data/certs/
        cp /certs/key.pem /data/certs/

        cat > /data/dynamic/rails.yml << 'EOFCONFIG'
        http:
          routers:
            rails-http:
              rule: "Host(`sandcastle.tc`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - web
            rails-https:
              rule: "Host(`sandcastle.tc`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - websecure
              tls: {}
          services:
            rails:
              loadBalancer:
                servers:
                  - url: "http://sandcastle-web:80"
                passHostHeader: true

        tls:
          certificates:
            - certFile: /data/certs/cert.pem
              keyFile: /data/certs/key.pem
        EOFCONFIG

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_production
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_production"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:local
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "${BUILD_VERSION:-dev}"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: "${BUILD_GIT_SHA:-local}"
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: "${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY:-false}"
        BUILD_DATE: "${BUILD_DATE:-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)}"
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    command: ["./bin/rails", "server", "-b", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "80"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: sandcastle.tc
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://sandcastle.tc:8443
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    image: sandcastle:local
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "${BUILD_VERSION:-dev}"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: "${BUILD_GIT_SHA:-local}"
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: "${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY:-false}"
        BUILD_DATE: "${BUILD_DATE:-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)}"
    container_name: sandcastle-worker
    command: ["bundle", "exec", "rake", "solid_queue:start"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: sandcastle.tc
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  migrate:
    image: sandcastle:local
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER: thies
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL: thieso@gmail.com
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD: tubu
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY: "ssh-ed25519 REDACTED thieso@gmail.com"
      SANDCASTLE_SKIP_PASSWORD_CHANGE: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

volumes:
  pgdata:
  sandcastle-data:
  traefik-data:

private

  SELFSIGNED_MODES = %w[selfsigned mkcert].freeze

  def write_tls_config
    tls_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "tls.yml")

    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
      # Traefik-perspective path (referenced inside tls.yml)
      cert_dir = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR", "/data/certs")
      # Rails-perspective path (where we can actually write files)
      local_cert_dir = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs")

      ensure_selfsigned_cert(local_cert_dir) if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"

      certs = [ { "certFile" => "#{cert_dir}/cert.pem", "keyFile" => "#{cert_dir}/key.pem" } ]

      if custom_cert_configured?
        certs << { "certFile" => "/data/certs/custom-cert.pem", "keyFile" => "/data/certs/custom-key.pem" }
      end

      File.write(tls_path, { "tls" => { "certificates" => certs } }.to_yaml)
    else
      File.delete(tls_path) if File.exist?(tls_path)
    end
  end

  def ensure_selfsigned_cert(cert_dir)
    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating self-signed certificate for #{host}")

    key  = OpenSSL::PKey::RSA.generate(4096)
    cert = OpenSSL::X509::Certificate.new
    cert.version    = 2
    cert.serial     = OpenSSL::BN.rand(128)
    cert.subject    = OpenSSL::X509::Name.parse("/CN=#{host}")
    cert.issuer     = cert.subject
    cert.public_key = key.public_key
    cert.not_before = Time.now
    cert.not_after  = Time.now + 10 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60

    ef = OpenSSL::X509::ExtensionFactory.new
    ef.subject_certificate = cert
    ef.issuer_certificate  = cert

    san = host.match?(/\A[\d.]+\z/) ? "IP:#{host}" : "DNS:#{host}"
    san += ",IP:127.0.0.1,DNS:localhost"
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("subjectAltName", san))
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("basicConstraints", "CA:FALSE", true))
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("keyUsage", "digitalSignature,keyEncipherment", true))
    cert.sign(key, OpenSSL::Digest::SHA256.new)

    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)
    File.write(key_path,  key.to_pem,  perm: 0o600)
    File.write(cert_path, cert.to_pem, perm: 0o644)
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: self-signed certificate written to #{cert_path}")
  end

  def custom_cert_configured?
    cert_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-cert.pem")
    key_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-key.pem")
    File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)
  end

  def config_path(sandbox)
    File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
  end

  def write_config(sandbox)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    routes = sandbox.routes.reload
    routers = {}
    services = {}

    routes.each do |route|

content.text:
Good. Here's what changed and why:

**`app/services/route_manager.rb`**

`write_tls_config` now calls `ensure_selfsigned_cert` (only in `selfsigned` mode) before writing `tls.yml`. `ensure_selfsigned_cert` uses Ruby's built-in `OpenSSL` module — no external tools needed — to generate a 4096-bit RSA key + self-signed cert and write them to `/data/traefik/certs/` (the Rails-side path on the shared volume). If the cert already exists (production: placed by the installer; subsequent restarts) it's a no-op.

Two paths are kept distinct:
- `local_cert_dir` = `/data/traefik/certs/` — where Rails **writes** files (its view of the shared volume)
- `cert_dir` = `/data/certs` — what Traefik **reads** in `tls.yml` (its view of the same volume)

**`docker-compose.local.yml`**

Removed the `.cert:/certs:ro` bind mount and the `cp /certs/*.pem` lines from `init-traefik`. You no longer need a `.cert/` directory or any manual cert generation step before running `mise run deploy:local`.

The startup sequence now is:
1. `init-traefik` creates dirs + writes placeholder `rails.yml`
2. Traefik starts (no cert yet, just watches for file changes)
3. Rails app starts → `write_rails_config` → `ensure_selfsigned_cert` generates cert → `write_tls_config` writes `tls.yml` → Traefik's file watcher picks it up and loads the cert

Currently:

The user wants `mkcert` mode to also auto-generate the cert via the `mkcert` binary (which must be installed on the host/container).

For the CA root directory, since the installer already sets it to the same location as the certificate directory, I can simplify by defaulting `MKCERT_CAROOT` to that same path rather than requiring a separate configuration. Writing the certificate setup code...

private

  SELFSIGNED_MODES = %w[selfsigned mkcert].freeze

  def write_tls_config
    tls_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "tls.yml")

    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
      # Traefik-perspective path (referenced inside tls.yml)
      cert_dir = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR", "/data/certs")
      # Rails-perspective path (where we can actually write files)
      local_cert_dir = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs")

      ensure_selfsigned_cert(local_cert_dir) if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"

      certs = [ { "certFile" => "#{cert_dir}/cert.pem", "keyFile" => "#{cert_dir}/key.pem" } ]

      if custom_cert_configured?
        certs << { "certFile" => "/data/certs/custom-cert.pem", "keyFile" => "/data/certs/custom-key.pem" }
      end

      File.write(tls_path, { "tls" => { "certificates" => certs } }.to_yaml)
    else
      File.delete(tls_path) if File.exist?(tls_path)
    end
  end

  def ensure_selfsigned_cert(cert_dir)
    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating self-signed certificate for #{host}")

    key  = OpenSSL::PKey::RSA.generate(4096)
    cert = OpenSSL::X509::Certificate.new
    cert.version    = 2
    cert.serial     = OpenSSL::BN.rand(128)
    cert.subject    = OpenSSL::X509::Name.parse("/CN=#{host}")
    cert.issuer     = cert.subject
    cert.public_key = key.public_key
    cert.not_before = Time.now
    cert.not_after  = Time.now + 10 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60

    ef = OpenSSL::X509::ExtensionFactory.new
    ef.subject_certificate = cert
    ef.issuer_certificate  = cert

    san = host.match?(/\A[\d.]+\z/) ? "IP:#{host}" : "DNS:#{host}"
    san += ",IP:127.0.0.1,DNS:localhost"
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("subjectAltName", san))
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("basicConstraints", "CA:FALSE", true))
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("keyUsage", "digitalSignature,keyEncipherment", true))
    cert.sign(key, OpenSSL::Digest::SHA256.new)

    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)
    File.write(key_path,  key.to_pem,  perm: 0o600)
    File.write(cert_path, cert.to_pem, perm: 0o644)
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: self-signed certificate written to #{cert_path}")
  end

  def custom_cert_configured?
    cert_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-cert.pem")
    key_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-key.pem")

class RouteManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  DYNAMIC_DIR = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")
  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"

  class Error < StandardError; end

  def add_route(sandbox:, domain:, port: 8080)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"

    route = sandbox.routes.create!(domain: domain, port: port)

    ensure_network
    connect_to_network(sandbox)
    write_config(sandbox)

    route
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
    raise Error, e.message
  end

  def remove_route(route:)
    sandbox = route.sandbox
    route.destroy!

    if sandbox.routes.reload.any?
      write_config(sandbox)
    else
      delete_config(sandbox)
      disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    end
  end

  def remove_all_routes(sandbox:)
    return unless sandbox.routed?

    sandbox.routes.destroy_all
    delete_config(sandbox)
    disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
  end

  def suspend_routes(sandbox:)
    return unless sandbox.routed?

    delete_config(sandbox)
    disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
  end

  def reconnect_routes(sandbox:)
    return unless sandbox.routed?

    ensure_network
    connect_to_network(sandbox)
    write_config(sandbox)
  end

  def sync_all_configs
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    active_ids = Sandbox.active.running.joins(:routes).distinct.pluck(:id).to_set

    # Remove stale config files
    Dir.glob(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-*.yml")).each do |path|
      id = File.basename(path, ".yml").delete_prefix("sandbox-").to_i
      unless active_ids.include?(id)
        File.delete(path)
        Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: removed stale config #{File.basename(path)}")
      end
    end

    # Regenerate configs for active routed sandboxes
    Sandbox.active.running.joins(:routes).distinct.includes(:user, :routes).find_each do |sandbox|
      ensure_network
      connect_to_network(sandbox)
      write_config(sandbox)
    end
  end

  def write_rails_config(host:)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    # Build host list: main host + optional alternative hostnames
    hosts = [ host ]
    alt_hostnames = ENV["SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES"].to_s.split(",").map(&:strip).reject(&:empty?)
    hosts += alt_hostnames

    host_rules = hosts.map { |h| "`#{h}`" }.join(", ")
    rule = "Host(#{host_rules})"

    config = {
      "http" => {
        "routers" => {
          "rails-http" => {
            "rule" => rule,
            "service" => "rails",
            "entryPoints" => [ "web" ]
          },
          "rails-https" => {
            "rule" => rule,
            "service" => "rails",
            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
            "tls" => tls_config
          }
        },
        "services" => {
          "rails" => {
            "loadBalancer" => {
              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://sandcastle-web:80" } ]
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }

    File.write(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "rails.yml"), config.to_yaml)
    write_tls_config
  end

  private

  SELFSIGNED_MODES = %w[selfsigned mkcert].freeze

  def write_tls_config
    tls_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "tls.yml")

    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
      # Traefik-perspective path (referenced inside tls.yml)
      cert_dir = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR", "/data/certs")
      # Rails-perspective path (where we can actually write files)
      local_cert_dir = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs")

      ensure_selfsigned_cert(local_cert_dir) if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"

      certs = [ { "certFile" => "#{cert_dir}/cert.pem", "keyFile" => "#{cert_dir}/key.pem" } ]

      if custom_cert_configured?
        certs << { "certFile" => "/data/certs/custom-cert.pem", "keyFile" => "/data/certs/custom-key.pem" }
      end

      File.write(tls_path, { "tls" => { "certificates" => certs } }.to_yaml)
    else
      File.delete(tls_path) if File.exist?(tls_path)
    end
  end

  def ensure_selfsigned_cert(cert_dir)
    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating self-signed certificate for #{host}")

    key  = OpenSSL::PKey::RSA.generate(4096)
    cert = OpenSSL::X509::Certificate.new
    cert.version    = 2
    cert.serial     = OpenSSL::BN.rand(128)
    cert.subject    = OpenSSL::X509::Name.parse("/CN=#{host}")
    cert.issuer     = cert.subject
    cert.public_key = key.public_key
    cert.not_before = Time.now
    cert.not_after  = Time.now + 10 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60

    ef = OpenSSL::X509::ExtensionFactory.new
    ef.subject_certificate = cert
    ef.issuer_certificate  = cert

    san = host.match?(/\A[\d.]+\z/) ? "IP:#{host}" : "DNS:#{host}"
    san += ",IP:127.0.0.1,DNS:localhost"
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("subjectAltName", san))
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("basicConstraints", "CA:FALSE", true))
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("keyUsage", "digitalSignature,keyEncipherment", true))
    cert.sign(key, OpenSSL::Digest::SHA256.new)

    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)
    File.write(key_path,  key.to_pem,  perm: 0o600)
    File.write(cert_path, cert.to_pem, perm: 0o644)
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: self-signed certificate written to #{cert_path}")
  end

  def custom_cert_configured?
    cert_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-cert.pem")
    key_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-key.pem")
    File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)
  end

  def config_path(sandbox)
    File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
  end

  def write_config(sandbox)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    routes = sandbox.routes.reload
    routers = {}
    services = {}

    routes.each do |route|
      key = "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}-r#{route.id}"
      routers[key] = {
        "rule" => "Host(`#{route.domain}`)",
        "service" => key,
        "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
        "tls" => tls_config
      }
      services[key] = {
        "loadBalancer" => {
          "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{sandbox.full_name}:#{route.port}" } ]
        }
      }
    end

    config = { "http" => { "routers" => routers, "services" => services } }
    File.write(config_path(sandbox), config.to_yaml)
  end

  def tls_config
    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
      {}
    else
      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
    end
  end

  def delete_config(sandbox)
    path = config_path(sandbox)
    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
  end

  def ensure_network
    Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Network.create(NETWORK_NAME, "Driver" => "bridge")
  end

  def connect_to_network(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)

    # Check if already connected
    networks = container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return if networks.key?(NETWORK_NAME)

    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to connect container to network: #{e.message}"
  end

  def disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    # Network or container already gone
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    # Container may not be connected
  end
end

def ensure_mkcert_cert(cert_dir)
    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating mkcert certificate for #{host}")

    # Store CA alongside the certs so it survives container restarts
    caroot = ENV.fetch("MKCERT_CAROOT", cert_dir)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)

    env = { "CAROOT" => caroot }
    system(env, "mkcert", "-install") or raise Error, "mkcert -install failed"
    system(
      env,
      "mkcert",
      "-cert-file", cert_path,
      "-key-file",  key_path,
      host, "*.#{host}", "localhost", "127.0.0.1", "::1"
    ) or raise Error, "mkcert certificate generation failed for #{host}"

    ca_source = File.join(caroot, "rootCA.pem")
    FileUtils.cp(ca_source, File.join(cert_dir, "rootCA.pem")) if File.exist?(ca_source)

    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: mkcert certificate written to #{cert_path}")
  end

  def custom_cert_configured?

    cert_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-cert.pem")
    key_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-key.pem")

content.text:
Done. Here's what was added:

**`ensure_mkcert_cert`** mirrors `ensure_selfsigned_cert` — idempotent (skips if cert already exists), shells out to `mkcert` with `CAROOT` set to the cert dir (so the CA is stored persistently alongside the cert, not in the default `~/.local/share/mkcert` which may not survive container restarts). After generation it copies `rootCA.pem` to the same dir for client distribution.

`MKCERT_CAROOT` env var overrides the CA location if needed.

**`write_tls_config`** now dispatches with a `case` instead of a bare `if`, so both modes auto-provision their certs symmetrically before writing `tls.yml`.

One thing to be aware of: `mkcert` must be present in the Rails container for this to work in `deploy:local`. In production the installer already runs `mkcert` on the host before the containers start, so the cert exists and the Rails code takes the early return — `mkcert` in the container is never called.

message.content:

content.thinking:
The user is quoting back my note about mkcert needing to be in the Rails container for `deploy:local`. They seem to be pointing out this is a problem or asking me to address it.

For `deploy:local`, mkcert won't be in the Rails container. The options are:
1. Add mkcert to the Dockerfile (so it's available in the Rails container)
2. Have an init container handle mkcert cert generation (like `init-traefik` but for mkcert)
3. Use a different approach for `deploy:local`

The cleanest solution is probably to add mkcert to the Dockerfile, since:


- The Dockerfile builds `sandcastle:local` for `deploy:local`, and adding mkcert there makes it available when needed
- The same Dockerfile is used for the production image published to GitHub Container Registry, so mkcert would be included there too
- It's a small binary and having it available in both environments makes sense

Let me check what's currently in the Dockerfile to see what we're working with.

file.content:
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
# check=error=true

# This Dockerfile is designed for production, not development. Use with Kamal or build'n'run by hand:
# docker build -t sandcastle .
# docker run -d -p 80:80 -e RAILS_MASTER_KEY=<value from config/master.key> --name sandcastle sandcastle

# For a containerized dev environment, see Dev Containers: https://guides.rubyonrails.org/getting_started_with_devcontainer.html

# Make sure RUBY_VERSION matches the Ruby version in .ruby-version
ARG RUBY_VERSION=4.0.0
FROM docker.io/library/ruby:$RUBY_VERSION-slim AS base

# Rails app lives here
WORKDIR /rails

# Install base packages
RUN apt-get update -qq && \
    apt-get install --no-install-recommends -y curl libjemalloc2 libvips libpq5 postgresql-client openssh-client && \
    ln -s /usr/lib/$(uname -m)-linux-gnu/libjemalloc.so.2 /usr/local/lib/libjemalloc.so && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists /var/cache/apt/archives

# Set production environment variables and enable jemalloc for reduced memory usage and latency.
ENV RAILS_ENV="production" \
    BUNDLE_DEPLOYMENT="1" \
    BUNDLE_PATH="/usr/local/bundle" \
    BUNDLE_WITHOUT="development" \
    LD_PRELOAD="/usr/local/lib/libjemalloc.so"

# Throw-away build stage to reduce size of final image
FROM base AS build

# Install packages needed to build gems
RUN apt-get update -qq && \
    apt-get install --no-install-recommends -y build-essential git libpq-dev libyaml-dev pkg-config && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists /var/cache/apt/archives

# Install application gems
COPY Gemfile Gemfile.lock vendor ./

RUN bundle install && \
    rm -rf ~/.bundle/ "${BUNDLE_PATH}"/ruby/*/cache "${BUNDLE_PATH}"/ruby/*/bundler/gems/*/.git && \
    # -j 1 disable parallel compilation to avoid a QEMU bug: https://github.com/rails/bootsnap/issues/495
    bundle exec bootsnap precompile -j 1 --gemfile

# Copy application code
COPY . .

# Precompile bootsnap code for faster boot times.
# -j 1 disable parallel compilation to avoid a QEMU bug: https://github.com/rails/bootsnap/issues/495
RUN bundle exec bootsnap precompile -j 1 app/ lib/

# Precompiling assets for production without requiring secret RAILS_MASTER_KEY
RUN SECRET_KEY_BASE_DUMMY=1 ./bin/rails assets:precompile


# Development stage for live source mounting
FROM base AS development

# Install development dependencies
RUN apt-get update -qq && \
    apt-get install --no-install-recommends -y \
    build-essential \
    git \
    libpq-dev \
    node-gyp \
    pkg-config \
    python-is-python3 \
    ca-certificates \
    gnupg && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists /var/cache/apt/archives

# Install Docker CLI
RUN install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings && \
    curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/debian/gpg -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc && \
    chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc && \
    echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc] https://download.docker.com/linux/debian $(. /etc/os-release && echo "$VERSION_CODENAME") stable" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list && \
    apt-get update -qq && \
    apt-get install --no-install-recommends -y docker-ce-cli && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists /var/cache/apt/archives

# Create user early
RUN groupadd --system --gid 220568 sandcastle && \
    useradd sandcastle --uid 220568 --gid 220568 --create-home --shell /bin/bash && \
    groupadd --system docker && \
    usermod -aG docker sandcastle

# Switch to development bundle config
ENV RAILS_ENV="development" \
    BUNDLE_PATH="/usr/local/bundle" \
    BUNDLE_WITHOUT=""

# Install all gems (including development/test)
COPY Gemfile Gemfile.lock ./
# Install libyaml-dev for psych gem
RUN apt-get update -qq && \
    apt-get install --no-install-recommends -y libyaml-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists /var/cache/apt/archives
RUN bundle install

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /rails

# Run as sandcastle user
USER 220568:220568

# Entrypoint for db readiness
ENTRYPOINT ["/rails/bin/docker-entrypoint"]

# Default to bin/dev (Foreman with Rails + Tailwind)
EXPOSE 80
CMD ["./bin/dev"]


# Final stage for app image
FROM base

# Install Docker CLI for container management via mounted socket
RUN apt-get update -qq && \
    apt-get install --no-install-recommends -y ca-certificates gnupg && \
    install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings && \
    curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/debian/gpg -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc && \
    chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc && \
    echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc] https://download.docker.com/linux/debian $(. /etc/os-release && echo "$VERSION_CODENAME") stable" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list && \
    apt-get update -qq && \
    apt-get install --no-install-recommends -y docker-ce-cli sudo btrfs-progs && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists /var/cache/apt/archives

# Run and own only the runtime files as a non-root user for security
RUN groupadd --system --gid 220568 sandcastle && \
    useradd sandcastle --uid 220568 --gid 220568 --create-home --shell /bin/bash && \
    groupadd --system docker && \
    usermod -aG docker sandcastle && \
    printf 'sandcastle ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/btrfs subvolume *\nsandcastle ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/chown\n' > /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle && \
    chmod 0440 /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle
USER 220568:220568

# Copy built artifacts: gems, application
COPY --chown=sandcastle:sandcastle --from=build "${BUNDLE_PATH}" "${BUNDLE_PATH}"
COPY --chown=sandcastle:sandcastle --from=build /rails /rails

# Build metadata for version footer
ARG BUILD_VERSION
ARG BUILD_GIT_SHA
ARG BUILD_GIT_DIRTY
ARG BUILD_DATE
ENV BUILD_VERSION=${BUILD_VERSION} \
    BUILD_GIT_SHA=${BUILD_GIT_SHA} \
    BUILD_GIT_DIRTY=${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY} \
    BUILD_DATE=${BUILD_DATE}

# Entrypoint prepares the database.
ENTRYPOINT ["/rails/bin/docker-entrypoint"]

# OCI labels for ghcr.io package linking
LABEL service="sandcastle"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/thieso2/sandcastle"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="Self-hosted shared Docker sandbox platform"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="MIT"

# Start server via Thruster by default, this can be overwritten at runtime
EXPOSE 80
CMD ["./bin/thrust", "./bin/rails", "server"]

content.thinking:
The solution is clear: install `mkcert` in the final stage of the Dockerfile (the production/local image used by `deploy:local`). This is the same stage where Docker CLI, sudo, and btrfs-progs are installed.

The `mkcert` binary download approach from the installer can be replicated in the Dockerfile. Since this is a multi-arch build (linux/amd64 and linux/arm64), we need to handle that properly.

The Dockerfile already uses `$(dpkg --print-architecture)` for Docker's apt repo. For mkcert, I'll use a similar approach with `uname -m` or `$(dpkg --print-architecture)`.


I can replicate the installer's architecture detection logic directly in the Dockerfile—mapping `uname -m` output to the correct mkcert architecture, then downloading and installing the binary from the filippo.io endpoint with the appropriate permissions.

However, Docker buildkit's `TARGETARCH` variable is cleaner since it already provides the architecture in mkcert's expected format (`amd64` or `arm64`), so I'll use that to download and install mkcert in the final stage after the Docker CLI setup.

The tricky part is that the final stage runs as user 220568, but `mkcert -install` needs root access to write to the system trust store, so I'll need to handle that permission requirement. But since we're running as non-root... hmm.

Actually, `mkcert -install` installs the CA into the system trust store. In a Docker container context for our use case, we don't actually need to install the CA into the container's trust store — we just need mkcert to generate the cert. The `-install` step is for making the CA trusted on client machines, not on the server.

Looking at the code that calls `mkcert -install`, it would fail if run as non-root since we can't write to the system CA store. We could skip `-install` entirely since Traefik only needs the cert file itself, not the CA in the system trust store. And mkcert will automatically create a CA in CAROOT if one doesn't exist when generating the first cert, so we don't need that installation step at all.

I'm writing the Ruby function that generates the certificate...

Now I need to add mkcert to the Dockerfile's final stage, but since it runs as a non-root user, I'll need to handle the installation carefully so the user can actually execute the binary. I'm settling on adding mkcert as a separate RUN step that downloads and installs it via curl before the USER directive, which keeps the root-level operations grouped together cleanly.

Now I need to update the `ensure_mkcert_cert` function to drop the `-install` flag since running as non-root would fail anyway, and the server doesn't need to install the CA into the system store.

content.text: The fix is in two parts: install `mkcert` in the Dockerfile's final stage, and drop the `mkcert -install` call from `ensure_mkcert_cert` (it installs the CA into the *system* trust store — a client-side operation that needs root and isn't needed on the server; mkcert auto-creates the CA in `CAROOT` when generating the first cert).

# Install mkcert for automatic cert generation in mkcert TLS mode

toolUseResult.originalFile:
class RouteManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  DYNAMIC_DIR = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")
  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"

  class Error < StandardError; end

  def add_route(sandbox:, domain:, port: 8080)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"

    route = sandbox.routes.create!(domain: domain, port: port)

    ensure_network
    connect_to_network(sandbox)
    write_config(sandbox)

    route
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
    raise Error, e.message
  end

  def remove_route(route:)
    sandbox = route.sandbox
    route.destroy!

    if sandbox.routes.reload.any?
      write_config(sandbox)
    else
      delete_config(sandbox)
      disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    end
  end

  def remove_all_routes(sandbox:)
    return unless sandbox.routed?

    sandbox.routes.destroy_all
    delete_config(sandbox)
    disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
  end

  def suspend_routes(sandbox:)
    return unless sandbox.routed?

    delete_config(sandbox)
    disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
  end

  def reconnect_routes(sandbox:)
    return unless sandbox.routed?

    ensure_network
    connect_to_network(sandbox)
    write_config(sandbox)
  end

  def sync_all_configs
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    active_ids = Sandbox.active.running.joins(:routes).distinct.pluck(:id).to_set

    # Remove stale config files
    Dir.glob(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-*.yml")).each do |path|
      id = File.basename(path, ".yml").delete_prefix("sandbox-").to_i
      unless active_ids.include?(id)
        File.delete(path)
        Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: removed stale config #{File.basename(path)}")
      end
    end

    # Regenerate configs for active routed sandboxes
    Sandbox.active.running.joins(:routes).distinct.includes(:user, :routes).find_each do |sandbox|
      ensure_network
      connect_to_network(sandbox)
      write_config(sandbox)
    end
  end

  def write_rails_config(host:)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    # Build host list: main host + optional alternative hostnames
    hosts = [ host ]
    alt_hostnames = ENV["SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES"].to_s.split(",").map(&:strip).reject(&:empty?)
    hosts += alt_hostnames

    host_rules = hosts.map { |h| "`#{h}`" }.join(", ")
    rule = "Host(#{host_rules})"

    config = {
      "http" => {
        "routers" => {
          "rails-http" => {
            "rule" => rule,
            "service" => "rails",
            "entryPoints" => [ "web" ]
          },
          "rails-https" => {
            "rule" => rule,
            "service" => "rails",
            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
            "tls" => tls_config
          }
        },
        "services" => {
          "rails" => {
            "loadBalancer" => {
              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://sandcastle-web:80" } ]
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }

    File.write(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "rails.yml"), config.to_yaml)
    write_tls_config
  end

  private

  SELFSIGNED_MODES = %w[selfsigned mkcert].freeze

  def write_tls_config
    tls_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "tls.yml")

    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
      # Traefik-perspective path (referenced inside tls.yml)
      cert_dir = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR", "/data/certs")
      # Rails-perspective path (where we can actually write files)
      local_cert_dir = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs")

      case ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"]
      when "selfsigned" then ensure_selfsigned_cert(local_cert_dir)
      when "mkcert"     then ensure_mkcert_cert(local_cert_dir)
      end

      certs = [ { "certFile" => "#{cert_dir}/cert.pem", "keyFile" => "#{cert_dir}/key.pem" } ]

      if custom_cert_configured?
        certs << { "certFile" => "/data/certs/custom-cert.pem", "keyFile" => "/data/certs/custom-key.pem" }
      end

      File.write(tls_path, { "tls" => { "certificates" => certs } }.to_yaml)
    else
      File.delete(tls_path) if File.exist?(tls_path)
    end
  end

  def ensure_selfsigned_cert(cert_dir)
    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating self-signed certificate for #{host}")

    key  = OpenSSL::PKey::RSA.generate(4096)
    cert = OpenSSL::X509::Certificate.new
    cert.version    = 2
    cert.serial     = OpenSSL::BN.rand(128)
    cert.subject    = OpenSSL::X509::Name.parse("/CN=#{host}")
    cert.issuer     = cert.subject
    cert.public_key = key.public_key
    cert.not_before = Time.now
    cert.not_after  = Time.now + 10 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60

    ef = OpenSSL::X509::ExtensionFactory.new
    ef.subject_certificate = cert
    ef.issuer_certificate  = cert

    san = host.match?(/\A[\d.]+\z/) ? "IP:#{host}" : "DNS:#{host}"
    san += ",IP:127.0.0.1,DNS:localhost"
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("subjectAltName", san))
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("basicConstraints", "CA:FALSE", true))
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("keyUsage", "digitalSignature,keyEncipherment", true))
    cert.sign(key, OpenSSL::Digest::SHA256.new)

    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)
    File.write(key_path,  key.to_pem,  perm: 0o600)
    File.write(cert_path, cert.to_pem, perm: 0o644)
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: self-signed certificate written to #{cert_path}")
  end

  def ensure_mkcert_cert(cert_dir)
    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating mkcert certificate for #{host}")

    # Store CA alongside the certs so it survives container restarts
    caroot = ENV.fetch("MKCERT_CAROOT", cert_dir)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)

    env = { "CAROOT" => caroot }
    system(env, "mkcert", "-install") or raise Error, "mkcert -install failed"
    system(
      env,
      "mkcert",
      "-cert-file", cert_path,
      "-key-file",  key_path,
      host, "*.#{host}", "localhost", "127.0.0.1", "::1"
    ) or raise Error, "mkcert certificate generation failed for #{host}"

    ca_source = File.join(caroot, "rootCA.pem")
    FileUtils.cp(ca_source, File.join(cert_dir, "rootCA.pem")) if File.exist?(ca_source)

    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: mkcert certificate written to #{cert_path}")
  end

  def custom_cert_configured?
    cert_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-cert.pem")
    key_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-key.pem")
    File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)
  end

  def config_path(sandbox)
    File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
  end

  def write_config(sandbox)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    routes = sandbox.routes.reload
    routers = {}
    services = {}

    routes.each do |route|
      key = "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}-r#{route.id}"
      routers[key] = {
        "rule" => "Host(`#{route.domain}`)",
        "service" => key,
        "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
        "tls" => tls_config
      }
      services[key] = {
        "loadBalancer" => {
          "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{sandbox.full_name}:#{route.port}" } ]
        }
      }
    end

    config = { "http" => { "routers" => routers, "services" => services } }
    File.write(config_path(sandbox), config.to_yaml)
  end

  def tls_config
    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
      {}
    else
      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
    end
  end

  def delete_config(sandbox)
    path = config_path(sandbox)
    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
  end

  def ensure_network
    Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Network.create(NETWORK_NAME, "Driver" => "bridge")
  end

  def connect_to_network(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)

    # Check if already connected
    networks = container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return if networks.key?(NETWORK_NAME)

    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to connect container to network: #{e.message}"
  end

  def disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    # Network or container already gone
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    # Container may not be connected
  end
end

content.text:
Two changes:

**`Dockerfile`** — added a `RUN` block (as root, before the `USER` switch) that downloads the `mkcert` binary from `dl.filippo.io` for the container's architecture and puts it at `/usr/local/bin/mkcert`. This applies to both `deploy:local` builds and the published production image.

**`route_manager.rb`** — dropped `mkcert -install`. That command installs the CA into the OS trust store (for browsers/curl on *that machine* to trust the cert). The server doesn't need to trust its own cert — only Traefik needs the cert *file*, which mkcert writes directly. mkcert will auto-create the CA keypair in `CAROOT` the first time it generates a cert, so `-install` was never needed here.

file.content:
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.3
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.address=:443"
      - "--providers.file.directory=/data/dynamic"
      - "--providers.file.watch=true"
      - "--log.level=INFO"
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic /data/certs
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data

        cat > /data/dynamic/rails.yml << 'EOFCONFIG'
        http:
          routers:
            rails-http:
              rule: "Host(`sandcastle.tc`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - web
            rails-https:
              rule: "Host(`sandcastle.tc`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - websecure
              tls: {}
          services:
            rails:
              loadBalancer:
                servers:
                  - url: "http://sandcastle-web:80"
                passHostHeader: true

        tls:
          certificates:
            - certFile: /data/certs/cert.pem
              keyFile: /data/certs/key.pem
        EOFCONFIG

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_production
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_production"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:local
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "${BUILD_VERSION:-dev}"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: "${BUILD_GIT_SHA:-local}"
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: "${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY:-false}"
        BUILD_DATE: "${BUILD_DATE:-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)}"
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    command: ["./bin/rails", "server", "-b", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "80"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: sandcastle.tc
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://sandcastle.tc:8443
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    image: sandcastle:local
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "${BUILD_VERSION:-dev}"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: "${BUILD_GIT_SHA:-local}"
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: "${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY:-false}"
        BUILD_DATE: "${BUILD_DATE:-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)}"
    container_name: sandcastle-worker
    command: ["bundle", "exec", "rake", "solid_queue:start"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: sandcastle.tc
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  migrate:
    image: sandcastle:local
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER: thies
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL: thieso@gmail.com
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD: tubu
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY: "ssh-ed25519 REDACTED thieso@gmail.com"
      SANDCASTLE_SKIP_PASSWORD_CHANGE: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

volumes:
  pgdata:
  sandcastle-data:
  traefik-data:

content.text:
There are two issues here:

1. **`init-traefik` is writing a static `rails.yml` with hardcoded TLS config** — but that's now the Rails app's job (`write_rails_config` → `write_tls_config`). The static one written by `init-traefik` has the cert block inline in `rails.yml` instead of in the separate `tls.yml` that `RouteManager` writes. This will conflict.

2. **`SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned`** — correct, but the `SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR` env implies the Rails-side cert path will be `/data/traefik/certs/`, which is right since `traefik-data` is mounted at `/data/traefik`.

The `init-traefik` command should just create the directories — the Rails app handles everything else:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.3
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.address=:443"
      - "--providers.file.directory=/data/dynamic"
      - "--providers.file.watch=true"
      - "--log.level=INFO"
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic /data/certs
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data

        cat > /data/dynamic/rails.yml << 'EOFCONFIG'
        http:
          routers:
            rails-http:
              rule: "Host(`sandcastle.tc`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - web
            rails-https:
              rule: "Host(`sandcastle.tc`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - websecure
              tls: {}
          services:
            rails:
              loadBalancer:
                servers:
                  - url: "http://sandcastle-web:80"
                passHostHeader: true

        tls:
          certificates:
            - certFile: /data/certs/cert.pem
              keyFile: /data/certs/key.pem
        EOFCONFIG

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_production
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_production"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:local
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "${BUILD_VERSION:-dev}"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: "${BUILD_GIT_SHA:-local}"
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: "${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY:-false}"
        BUILD_DATE: "${BUILD_DATE:-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)}"
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    command: ["./bin/rails", "server", "-b", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "80"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: sandcastle.tc
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://sandcastle.tc:8443
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    image: sandcastle:local
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "${BUILD_VERSION:-dev}"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: "${BUILD_GIT_SHA:-local}"
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: "${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY:-false}"
        BUILD_DATE: "${BUILD_DATE:-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)}"
    container_name: sandcastle-worker
    command: ["bundle", "exec", "rake", "solid_queue:start"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: sandcastle.tc
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  migrate:
    image: sandcastle:local
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER: thies
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL: thieso@gmail.com
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD: tubu
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY: "ssh-ed25519 REDACTED thieso@gmail.com"
      SANDCASTLE_SKIP_PASSWORD_CHANGE: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

volumes:
  pgdata:
  sandcastle-data:
  traefik-data:

content.text:
The `init-traefik` is now just a directory setup step. The Rails app writes `rails.yml` + `tls.yml` (and generates the cert) on startup via the `traefik.rb` initializer.

One remaining thing: Traefik starts before the Rails app, so for a brief moment it has an empty `/data/dynamic/` directory. That's fine — it'll serve a 404 until Rails writes the config files, then the file watcher picks them up within a second.

From the docker-compose.local.yml I just read:
- Hostname: `sandcastle.tc` (hardcoded in SANDCASTLE_HOST env var)
- HTTP port: 8080 (maps to container port 80)
- HTTPS port: 8443 (maps to container port 443)
- TLS mode: selfsigned
- Terminal URL: https://sandcastle.tc:8443
- Admin user: thies


- Admin email: thieso@gmail.com
- Admin password: tubu

To get this running locally, they'll need to add `sandcastle.tc` to their `/etc/hosts` file pointing to `127.0.0.1`, create the external `sandcastle-web` Docker network, and then access the terminal at https://sandcastle.tc:8443 with those credentials.

content.text:
From the compose file:

- **Hostname:** `sandcastle.tc` (hardcoded in `SANDCASTLE_HOST`)
- **HTTP:** `http://localhost:8080` → redirects to HTTPS
- **HTTPS:** `https://sandcastle.tc:8443`
- **TLS mode:** `selfsigned` (cert auto-generated by the app on first boot)
- **Admin login:** `thieso@gmail.com` / `tubu`

**Prerequisites before `mise run deploy:local`:**

```bash
# 1. Add to /etc/hosts
echo "127.0.0.1 sandcastle.tc" | sudo tee -a /etc/hosts

# 2. Create the external network (only once)
docker network create sandcastle-web
```

Then:
```bash
mise run deploy:local
# → https://sandcastle.tc:8443
```

The `sandcastle-web` network is declared `external: true` because in production it's created by the installer and shared with sandbox/WeTTY containers. For local dev you just create it manually once.

```

Since the cert already exists, `ensure_selfsigned_cert` (and `ensure_mkcert_cert`) return early without regenerating. The existing mkcert cert doesn't have the right SAN for `dev.sand` (or `sandcastle.tc`), so Traefik can't match it to the SNI and falls back to the default cert.


The solution is to delete the old cert and restart the web container so it regenerates with the correct hostname. Since `SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE` is set to `selfsigned`, the app will create a new self-signed cert with the proper SANs for the current setup.

content.text:

Since the cert files already exist, `ensure_selfsigned_cert` returns early and never regenerates. Traefik's SNI matching rejects the mismatched cert and falls back to its default.

Fix: delete the stale cert and restart the web container to regenerate it for `dev.sand`:

content.thinking:
The user wants to debug why curl is failing with "unable to get local issuer certificate" and fix it. This is a self-signed cert issue - curl doesn't trust the CA because the CA isn't in the system trust store.

The current setup uses `selfsigned` mode which generates a self-signed cert using Ruby's OpenSSL. The cert is self-signed (its own CA), so curl can't verify it because the issuer (CA) isn't trusted.

For curl to work without warnings, we have two options:
1. Use `mkcert` mode - which creates a proper CA that can be installed on the machine
2. Add the CA cert to the system trust store (but for a self-signed cert, the cert IS the CA)


3. Add the self-signed cert directly to the macOS keychain as a trusted root certificate, or switch to `mkcert` mode in the docker-compose configuration to use a proper CA that can be installed system-wide.

The `mkcert` approach is cleaner since it's designed exactly for this—I'd need to update the environment variable, rebuild the Docker image to include the mkcert binary, clear out the old certificates, restart the services, and then install the generated CA on the host machine so curl trusts it automatically. the Docker containers are running Linux, so `uname -m` will return `aarch64` or `x86_64` depending on the architecture, which the sed command correctly converts to `arm64` or `amd64`. So my plan is to update the TLS mode to `mkcert` in the compose file, rebuild the image, delete the old certificates, restart the web container to generate new ones, extract the root CA certificate to the host, install it in macOS's keychain, and then test it with curl. But there's something I need to check...

Looking at the `ensure_mkcert_cert` function more closely...

Examining the certificate generation logic...

Finishing the mkcert command...

The rootCA.pem will end up at `/data/traefik/certs/rootCA.pem` and can be extracted from the container using `docker exec`. I'm checking whether mkcert will work as user 220568 inside the container — the binary is installed at `/usr/local/bin/mkcert` and should be executable by that user. Since we're not installing the CA system-wide (we removed the `-install` flag), this doesn't require elevated privileges. The approach is straightforward: update the docker-compose config to use mkcert mode, rebuild the image, clear the old certs from the volume, and restart the service.

Now I'm deciding between two paths — either extract the self-signed cert and add it to the macOS keychain as trusted, or stick with the mkcert approach. The mkcert solution is cleaner since that's what it's designed for, and we already have the infrastructure in place. Let me implement this step by step and iterate until curl works without warnings.

I need to switch the TLS mode to mkcert in the docker-compose file, rebuild the web service, remove the old cert files, restart to generate the new mkcert certificate, copy the rootCA.pem to the host, and then install it on macOS. The tricky part is that the CA gets generated inside the container, so I need to figure out how to extract it and install it properly on the host machine.

content.text: The cert is self-signed with no trusted CA — curl can't verify it. The fix: switch `deploy:local` to `mkcert` mode, which creates a proper local CA. We install that CA on the Mac once, then curl (and browsers) trust all certs it signs.

class RouteManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  DYNAMIC_DIR = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")
  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"

  class Error < StandardError; end

  def add_route(sandbox:, domain:, port: 8080)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"

    route = sandbox.routes.create!(domain: domain, port: port)

    ensure_network
    connect_to_network(sandbox)
    write_config(sandbox)

    route
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
    raise Error, e.message
  end

  def remove_route(route:)
    sandbox = route.sandbox
    route.destroy!

    if sandbox.routes.reload.any?
      write_config(sandbox)
    else
      delete_config(sandbox)
      disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    end
  end

  def remove_all_routes(sandbox:)
    return unless sandbox.routed?

    sandbox.routes.destroy_all
    delete_config(sandbox)
    disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
  end

  def suspend_routes(sandbox:)
    return unless sandbox.routed?

    delete_config(sandbox)
    disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
  end

  def reconnect_routes(sandbox:)
    return unless sandbox.routed?

    ensure_network
    connect_to_network(sandbox)
    write_config(sandbox)
  end

  def sync_all_configs
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    active_ids = Sandbox.active.running.joins(:routes).distinct.pluck(:id).to_set

    # Remove stale config files
    Dir.glob(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-*.yml")).each do |path|
      id = File.basename(path, ".yml").delete_prefix("sandbox-").to_i
      unless active_ids.include?(id)
        File.delete(path)
        Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: removed stale config #{File.basename(path)}")
      end
    end

    # Regenerate configs for active routed sandboxes
    Sandbox.active.running.joins(:routes).distinct.includes(:user, :routes).find_each do |sandbox|
      ensure_network
      connect_to_network(sandbox)
      write_config(sandbox)
    end
  end

  def write_rails_config(host:)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    # Build host list: main host + optional alternative hostnames
    hosts = [ host ]
    alt_hostnames = ENV["SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES"].to_s.split(",").map(&:strip).reject(&:empty?)
    hosts += alt_hostnames

    host_rules = hosts.map { |h| "`#{h}`" }.join(", ")
    rule = "Host(#{host_rules})"

    config = {
      "http" => {
        "routers" => {
          "rails-http" => {
            "rule" => rule,
            "service" => "rails",
            "entryPoints" => [ "web" ]
          },
          "rails-https" => {
            "rule" => rule,
            "service" => "rails",
            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
            "tls" => tls_config
          }
        },
        "services" => {
          "rails" => {
            "loadBalancer" => {
              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://sandcastle-web:80" } ]
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }

    File.write(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "rails.yml"), config.to_yaml)
    write_tls_config
  end

  private

  SELFSIGNED_MODES = %w[selfsigned mkcert].freeze

  def write_tls_config
    tls_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "tls.yml")

    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
      # Traefik-perspective path (referenced inside tls.yml)
      cert_dir = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR", "/data/certs")
      # Rails-perspective path (where we can actually write files)
      local_cert_dir = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs")

      case ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"]
      when "selfsigned" then ensure_selfsigned_cert(local_cert_dir)
      when "mkcert"     then ensure_mkcert_cert(local_cert_dir)
      end

      certs = [ { "certFile" => "#{cert_dir}/cert.pem", "keyFile" => "#{cert_dir}/key.pem" } ]

      if custom_cert_configured?
        certs << { "certFile" => "/data/certs/custom-cert.pem", "keyFile" => "/data/certs/custom-key.pem" }
      end

      File.write(tls_path, { "tls" => { "certificates" => certs } }.to_yaml)
    else
      File.delete(tls_path) if File.exist?(tls_path)
    end
  end

  def ensure_selfsigned_cert(cert_dir)
    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating self-signed certificate for #{host}")

    key  = OpenSSL::PKey::RSA.generate(4096)
    cert = OpenSSL::X509::Certificate.new
    cert.version    = 2
    cert.serial     = OpenSSL::BN.rand(128)
    cert.subject    = OpenSSL::X509::Name.parse("/CN=#{host}")
    cert.issuer     = cert.subject
    cert.public_key = key.public_key
    cert.not_before = Time.now
    cert.not_after  = Time.now + 10 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60

    ef = OpenSSL::X509::ExtensionFactory.new
    ef.subject_certificate = cert
    ef.issuer_certificate  = cert

    san = host.match?(/\A[\d.]+\z/) ? "IP:#{host}" : "DNS:#{host}"
    san += ",IP:127.0.0.1,DNS:localhost"
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("subjectAltName", san))
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("basicConstraints", "CA:FALSE", true))
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("keyUsage", "digitalSignature,keyEncipherment", true))
    cert.sign(key, OpenSSL::Digest::SHA256.new)

    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)
    File.write(key_path,  key.to_pem,  perm: 0o600)
    File.write(cert_path, cert.to_pem, perm: 0o644)
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: self-signed certificate written to #{cert_path}")
  end

  def ensure_mkcert_cert(cert_dir)
    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating mkcert certificate for #{host}")

    # Store CA alongside the certs so it survives container restarts
    caroot = ENV.fetch("MKCERT_CAROOT", cert_dir)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)

    env = { "CAROOT" => caroot }
    system(
      env,
      "mkcert",
      "-cert-file", cert_path,
      "-key-file",  key_path,
      host, "*.#{host}", "localhost", "127.0.0.1", "::1"
    ) or raise Error, "mkcert certificate generation failed for #{host}"

    ca_source = File.join(caroot, "rootCA.pem")
    FileUtils.cp(ca_source, File.join(cert_dir, "rootCA.pem")) if File.exist?(ca_source)

    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: mkcert certificate written to #{cert_path}")
  end

  def custom_cert_configured?
    cert_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-cert.pem")
    key_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-key.pem")
    File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)
  end

  def config_path(sandbox)
    File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
  end

  def write_config(sandbox)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    routes = sandbox.routes.reload
    routers = {}
    services = {}

    routes.each do |route|
      key = "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}-r#{route.id}"
      routers[key] = {
        "rule" => "Host(`#{route.domain}`)",
        "service" => key,
        "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
        "tls" => tls_config
      }
      services[key] = {
        "loadBalancer" => {
          "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{sandbox.full_name}:#{route.port}" } ]
        }
      }
    end

    config = { "http" => { "routers" => routers, "services" => services } }
    File.write(config_path(sandbox), config.to_yaml)
  end

  def tls_config
    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
      {}
    else
      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
    end
  end

  def delete_config(sandbox)
    path = config_path(sandbox)
    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
  end

  def ensure_network
    Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Network.create(NETWORK_NAME, "Driver" => "bridge")
  end

  def connect_to_network(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)

    # Check if already connected
    networks = container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return if networks.key?(NETWORK_NAME)

    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to connect container to network: #{e.message}"
  end

  def disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    # Network or container already gone
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    # Container may not be connected
  end
end

content.text:
`SSL certificate verify ok.` — curl is happy. The full working flow for `mkcert` mode in `deploy:local` is now:

1. `mise run deploy:local` — app auto-generates a mkcert CA + cert on first boot
2. Extract and install the CA once:
   ```bash
   docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml exec web cat /data/traefik/certs/rootCA.pem > /tmp/ca.pem
   sudo security add-trusted-cert -d -r trustRoot -k /Library/Keychains/System.keychain /tmp/ca.pem
   ```
3. All subsequent runs reuse the same CA (it's persisted in the `traefik-data` volume), so no reinstall needed until the volume is wiped.

file.content:
class TailscaleController < ApplicationController
  def show
    if Current.user.tailscale_enabled?
      @status = TailscaleManager.new.status(user: Current.user)
    elsif Current.user.tailscale_pending?
      @pending = true
    end
  rescue TailscaleManager::Error => e
    @error = e.message
  end

  def login
    tag = params[:tailscale_tag].presence
    Rails.cache.write("ts_tag:#{Current.user.id}", tag, expires_in: 10.minutes) if tag
    TailscaleManager.new.start_login(user: Current.user)
    redirect_to tailscale_path, status: :see_other
  rescue TailscaleManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    flash[:alert] = e.message
    redirect_to tailscale_path, status: :see_other
  end

  def login_status
    result = TailscaleManager.new.check_login(user: Current.user)
    render json: result
  rescue TailscaleManager::Error => e
    render json: { status: "error", error: e.message }, status: :unprocessable_entity
  end

  def update_settings
    Current.user.update!(tailscale_auto_connect: params[:auto_connect] == "1")
    redirect_to tailscale_path, notice: "Settings updated"
  end

  def disable
    TailscaleManager.new.disable(user: Current.user)
    redirect_to tailscale_path, notice: "Tailscale disabled"
  rescue TailscaleManager::Error => e
    redirect_to tailscale_path, alert: e.message
  end
end

content.thinking:
The key is in `_pending.html.erb` line 100: when `authenticated`, the JS does `window.location.reload()`. This reloads the Tailscale page, which then shows the connected state. Instead, we should redirect to the dashboard (root path) with a flash message.

But we can't set a flash message from JavaScript with `window.location.reload()`. We need to redirect to a URL that sets the flash.

Two approaches:
1. Change `window.location.reload()` to `window.location.href = '/some-path-that-sets-flash'` - but that requires a new action


2. Change the JS to redirect to a query parameter and handle it in the controller

The cleanest solution is to add a dedicated `connected` action to the TailscaleController that sets the flash and redirects to root, then update the JS to point there instead of reloading.

Now I'm checking the current route structure to see how it's organized.

The fix:

2. Add a `connected` action to `TailscaleController` that sets a flash notice and redirects to `root_path`
3. Change the JS in `_pending.html.erb` from `window.location.reload()` to redirect to the `connected_tailscale_path`

Also, I should check if there's an auth key flow (not just interactive login) that also needs the flash. Looking at the `Api::TailscaleController`:

Rails.application.routes.draw do

  post "invites/:token/accept", to: "registrations#create", as: :accept_registration

  get  "auth/:provider/callback", to: "oauth_callbacks#create"
  post "auth/:provider/callback", to: "oauth_callbacks#create"
  get  "auth/failure",            to: "oauth_callbacks#failure"
  resource :change_password, only: [ :show, :update ]

  resource :settings, only: :show do
    patch :update_profile
    patch :update_password
    patch :toggle_tailscale

    delete "revoke_token/:id", action: :revoke_token, as: :revoke_token
  end

  get  "auth/device",              to: "device_auth#show",     as: :auth_device
  post "auth/device/verify",       to: "device_auth#verify",   as: :auth_device_verify

  resource :tailscale, only: [], controller: "tailscale" do

class TailscaleController < ApplicationController
  def show
    if Current.user.tailscale_enabled?
      @status = TailscaleManager.new.status(user: Current.user)
    elsif Current.user.tailscale_pending?
      @pending = true
    end
  rescue TailscaleManager::Error => e
    @error = e.message
  end

  def login
    tag = params[:tailscale_tag].presence
    Rails.cache.write("ts_tag:#{Current.user.id}", tag, expires_in: 10.minutes) if tag
    TailscaleManager.new.start_login(user: Current.user)
    redirect_to tailscale_path, status: :see_other
  rescue TailscaleManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    flash[:alert] = e.message
    redirect_to tailscale_path, status: :see_other
  end

  def login_status
    result = TailscaleManager.new.check_login(user: Current.user)
    render json: result
  rescue TailscaleManager::Error => e
    render json: { status: "error", error: e.message }, status: :unprocessable_entity
  end

  def update_settings
    Current.user.update!(tailscale_auto_connect: params[:auto_connect] == "1")
    redirect_to tailscale_path, notice: "Settings updated"
  end

  def disable
    TailscaleManager.new.disable(user: Current.user)
    redirect_to tailscale_path, notice: "Tailscale disabled"
  rescue TailscaleManager::Error => e
    redirect_to tailscale_path, alert: e.message
  end
end
